text:
FDG PET/CT - WHOLE BODY (SKULL BASE TO MID-THIGH)

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Chen, 52-year-old female. HPV-positive (p16+) squamous cell carcinoma of the left base of tongue, T2N1M0 per AJCC 8th edition. Patient completed definitive concurrent chemoradiotherapy (70 Gy IMRT over 35 fractions with weekly cisplatin) approximately 12 weeks prior to this examination. This study is obtained for post-treatment response assessment.

COMPARISON:
Prior staging FDG PET/CT performed prior to initiation of treatment. Prior MRI neck with contrast performed in conjunction with the staging workup.

TECHNIQUE:
Whole-body FDG PET/CT was performed following intravenous administration of 10.8 mCi of F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG). The patient fasted for greater than 6 hours prior to injection. Serum glucose at time of injection: 88 mg/dL. Uptake time: 63 minutes. CT was performed from skull base to mid-thighs with intravenous contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL, power injected) for attenuation correction and anatomic localization. Non-diagnostic low-dose CT technique employed for attenuation correction. PET images were acquired in 3D mode with iterative reconstruction. Fused PET/CT images were reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK - PRIMARY SITE:
The left base of tongue is evaluated carefully in comparison to the pre-treatment staging PET/CT. Previously, there was an intensely FDG-avid mass at the left base of tongue with an SUVmax of 14.2. On this examination, there is no discrete FDG-avid lesion identifiable at the primary site. The base of tongue demonstrates mildly increased diffuse background uptake (SUVmax 2.1), which is symmetric and within the range expected for post-radiation inflammatory and mucosal healing changes at this interval. No focal or nodular area of increased uptake to suggest residual or recurrent metabolically active tumor. On the CT component, there is mild mucosal thickening at the left base of tongue without a discrete enhancing mass, consistent with post-radiation mucosal changes. The pre-epiglottic space and vallecula appear without abnormal density or FDG activity.

HEAD AND NECK - NODAL DISEASE:
The previously identified left level IIA lymph node, which measured 2.5 cm and had an SUVmax of 11.8 on the pre-treatment study, has markedly decreased in size. On the CT component, a small residual oval soft tissue density measuring approximately 8 x 6 mm is identified at the left level IIA station. This structure demonstrates no FDG hypermetabolism, with an SUVmax of 1.8, which is at the level of background mediastinal blood pool activity. There is no internal necrosis or irregular margins on CT. The findings are entirely consistent with treatment-related fibrosis and nodal involution rather than viable nodal metastasis.

The remaining cervical lymph node stations are surveyed bilaterally. No FDG-avid lymph nodes are identified at levels I, IB, IIA, IIB, III, IV, or VA/VB bilaterally. No contralateral cervical adenopathy. No new retropharyngeal, parapharyngeal, or posterior triangle lymphadenopathy. Several small lymph nodes in the submandibular and upper cervical regions measuring up to 7 mm are noted bilaterally; these are not FDG-avid and are not enlarged by CT size criteria, consistent with reactive or normal nodes.

HEAD AND NECK - OTHER STRUCTURES:
The oropharynx, hypopharynx, and larynx demonstrate expected post-radiation mucosal changes without focal areas of abnormal FDG uptake to suggest recurrence. No abnormal FDG activity in the nasopharynx or oral cavity. Salivary glands: Diffusely decreased FDG uptake in the bilateral parotid glands compared to the pre-treatment study, consistent with post-radiation glandular changes and reduced secretory activity. Thyroid gland: Mild heterogeneous activity; no focal photopenic or hypermetabolic lesion. Mandible: No FDG-avid focus along the mandible to suggest osteoradionecrosis or osseous involvement.

THORAX:
Lungs: The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or areas of consolidation are identified that would be suspicious for metastatic disease or a secondary primary. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. Mediastinum: No FDG-avid mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The mediastinal contour is within normal limits. No anterior mediastinal masses. Cardiac silhouette: Normal size and configuration without pericardial effusion. No abnormal FDG activity in the mediastinum or hila beyond physiologic vascular uptake.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
Liver: The liver is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous background uptake. No focal hypermetabolic lesion to suggest metastatic disease. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation on CT.
Spleen: Normal size and FDG activity.
Pancreas: No focal FDG-avid lesion.
Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands without FDG-avid lesion; no CT evidence of adrenal mass.
Kidneys: Bilateral kidneys with physiologic FDG excretion. No focal renal lesion identified.
Bowel: Physiologic bowel activity without focal area of concern. Stomach: No abnormal activity.
Pelvic organs: Uterus and adnexa without FDG-avid lesion. Bladder: Physiologic tracer activity.
Peritoneum: No ascites. No FDG-avid peritoneal implants.

SKELETAL SURVEY:
No FDG-avid osseous lesion to suggest skeletal metastasis. The visualized axial and appendicular skeleton is without focal lytic or blastic abnormality on CT. Normal bone marrow FDG distribution without focal photopenic or hypermetabolic lesion.

OTHER:
No abnormal superficial or subcutaneous FDG-avid foci. No evidence of a synchronous second primary malignancy identified in the surveyed region. Incidental note of mild degenerative changes in the lower cervical spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complete resolution of the previously documented intensely FDG-avid left base of tongue primary tumor. The current mild, diffuse increased uptake at the primary site (SUVmax 2.1) is symmetric and at the level expected for post-treatment mucosal inflammation and healing at this interval; no focal nodular hypermetabolism to suggest residual viable tumor.

2. The previously hypermetabolic left level IIA cervical lymph node has dramatically decreased in size (now approximately 8 mm, from 25 mm) and shows no FDG uptake above background (SUVmax 1.8). The findings are consistent with effective treatment response and nodal fibrosis.

3. No new FDG-avid locoregional, cervical, mediastinal, or distant disease. No evidence of distant metastatic spread in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.

4. No synchronous second primary malignancy identified.

5. Incidental post-radiation changes in the parotid glands bilaterally, consistent with the known treatment history.

Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Nuclear Medicine/Head and Neck Imaging]
Dictated and verified electronically.
